transcript.text: Hey, Mithil.
How's it going?
Hey, Tyler.
How are you?
I'm doing well, sir.
How's everything going on your end?
Perfect.
Perfect.
We are making good progress.
I think my legal is reviewing the MSA and the SoW edits.
So I should get some response back by end of day today.
A couple of other things.
think the security side, they're also validating all the submissions.
And me and Nima both notified that you will need an access to our CMS atmosphere environment in general to provide review and provide suggestions and solutions, et cetera, things like that.
Yeah, correct.
And this is our VP of operations, Bridget.
I asked her to join today.
How are you?
Nice to meet you.
Nice to meet you as well.
Yes, our preference is to be able to stage and publish in your CMS.
But if that's going to invite more complexity or more hurdles we need to jump through, then we can let your team publish.
Yeah, not in regards to any kind of issues in general, because we have to explore it until I can tell you there is an issue.
So I think our privacy council is looking at it as a parallel workflow.
So I think we have multiple people doing the reviews, so we should have some solution by end of day.
Luckily, nothing is sequential or dependent on any other approver, so everything is parallel in general.
The only thing, Tyler, I have from finance is, well, two things.
Right now, one is the order form is basically encompassing 15 months, if I'm not wrong, correct?
Like based on the...
14, yeah.
Yeah, like eight weeks and then 12.
Got it, got it.
Because we were basically looking at this starts on November 1st, which is our start of Q4.
Phase one ends all the way till January 31st.
The reason for that is we want to capture the entire Q4 over here.
Plus, there will be quiet weeks and holidays, so there will be a miss out period.
So we want that phase one start from Q1, sorry, from November 1, all the way till January 31st.
So that's one.

And then the second one would be to actually the phase two, based on, let's say, by 15th of Jan or something, the second or third week, NEMA should be able to provide more kind of a good feedback on how things are progressing, what needs to change.
And that would lead to a continuation of phase two, which will be, again, an annual 12-month kind of a period agreement.

Initially, when I spoke to you, I told you that, like, because it's a net new vendor, we want to do it, like, either a complete 12 months, or break it down to phase one as PO1, and then phase two as a complete one-year PO, which is starting from Fev1, all the way till the next following year of January.
Yeah, the ask here, Bridget, is...
Bridget, is...
For us to be able to have Phase 2 as 12 months, we would have to have a completely separate contract because Navon, with new vendors, they only signed 12-month agreements.
So the ask is, in order to keep it on one document, can we do three months for Phase 1 and nine months for Phase 2?
Is that right, Mithil?
That's correct.
And I actually wanted to put some more light over there, Tyler, because there is a conditional kind of a clause of trying to see, okay, can we proceed or not?
I'm trying to validate with our operations team if we can break this down into two different purchase orders.
So technically, even though we are on the one single contract, but they are accounting two different purchase orders altogether.
So hopefully that would allow me to actually find a way around the process.
Okay.
Done.
Again, no kind of a promise is there.
That's what I'm juggling into.
What I'm struggling right now is from a budgeting perspective.
Right now, have Phase 1 allocations of 30,000 and total by...
Based on what we have spent so far around same services, 153 or something, kind of like spent allocated to Nima for this from an annual perspective.
So I wanted to check with you.
I mean, I can get this approved for the entire 15-month period.
I'll just have to find a way around to navigate around that our mandate of not doing more than 12 months internally by creating two POS, etc.
So that's my solve.
But if not, then we'll only, we basically need to break this down across two different agreements.
List one for 30K for phase one.
And then based on what budget gets approved, where we land together, we can do a different contract all together starting February.
So I wanted to see if there is any kind of a pathway for us to solve it at 153K for the entire phase one and phase two.
And if that's there, then I can get my finance approved right before end of day-to-day.
So that hurdle is gone.
Okay, so just to make sure I've got this right, you have a budget limitation of $153,000 total.
Yep.
If we can fit in that budget, we can structure this as one agreement, three months in phase one, 12 months in phase two.
Correct.
If we can't fit in that budget, what would our option be?
We'll have to figure it out.
mean, to be honest, I don't have a direct option.
I'm checking in with Nima as well.
The easiest option would be not having further more delays on the phase one.
want to get that started as early as possible.
So let's stand alone.
Let's get this phase one started.
Let's look if we can get that at $30,000 based on what we have.
I think the main narrative here from finance is the Q4 is usually super light.
A lot of people are off, so they want to make sure that we're getting the best kind of ROI on what we are investing.
Holidays, quiet week, etc.
Things like that.
So we'll stand alone do the phase one, get it done.

Coming back in January, Nima, me, we'll work with finance to find, okay, what kind of, if there is any incremental budget they can provide us based on the ROI, if there is any kind of other leeway for us to exercise during that period and go from there.
So again, we'll be going back to the exploratory board.
Luckily, at that time, our legal frameworks and all the addendums would be already there, so that would be a little faster.
But for finance, because our fiscal starts from Feb 1, we may or may not have additional budget.
It all depends on how we go through the Phase 1 and if there are any incremental budgets getting added to Nima's team for the Fiscal 27.
And also, Mithil, if we did three months for Phase 1 and nine months for Phase 2 and one agreement, and we were...
Yeah, should be...
mean, that's always going to be a green light, Tyler.
What I was trying to do is, okay, making like a pure kind of like justice in terms of looking back and then analyzing the performance.
Phase one is really the foundational basis of it where we are laying the land, putting the KPIs and the parameters in place.
And then we have the entire 12-month period to actually go through every single quarter to see how the seasonality is affecting the results.
So that's where I was working with Neema to see if we can find a parallel way around this process.
12 months, 9 plus 3 is always going to be a green light.
I mean, there is no kind of restrictions over that.
So, again, I'm going to put this on your side.
Happy to get this around 15 months.
Again, this is all coming from Neema having me solve this for the entire 15 months rather than breaking it down across two different agreements.
So he's trying to save his time for him and his team of not going through the entire process again and seeing if we are able to solve it.
So, again, we have multiple options here.
First, I mean, the main hurdle is really how the budget, we can solve for the budget.
And based on that, we can figure out, okay, what's the best way forward to quickly launch?
Because Nima told me to solve this before end of this week, at least for phase one.
I'm rushing internally to get all necessary approval.
So his team is like completely equipped with what he needs.
And we're all like ground running on November 1st kind of a thing.
So I hear what you're saying.
And you're saying you need 30K in phase one, right?
That's like a green light for me right away.
If you're not getting like, I know, I know, like we are looking at 40 and 30 and we can do the payment upfront for that.
also confirmed on that on the 30K for any additional need between that 30 to 40K.
I'll need to work with finance and Nima to see, okay, where the money can come from.
Just like do some kind of internal kind of like, again, this is good feedback in general.
So I'm super transparent on how things are moving and just want to be super candid there.
I think we're good on phase one, but phase two, if it's 153 total, that only leaves us 100.
23 left.
And right now we're at 180.
So that's a really substantial discount.
Yeah.
mean, and again, like typically what we paid for this program, we tried like multiple services in past.
So sometimes we paid 8,000.
Sometimes we are paid 10.5.
The maximum we have paid is 11.
So based on all that kind of a spend structured, that's where that total is coming from.
I will go back to finance team and see, okay, the 15K is their price.
What we're asking is somewhere around 10.5-ish, if I'm not wrong, based on that remainder total.
Is there any legal room around this to meet somewhere in the center and can come back to you?
Probably by tomorrow afternoon at max.
Bridget, do you have a preference on a nine-month phase two at the standard pricing or a discount for 12 months in phase two?
I can tell nine months.
Sorry to interrupt, Bridget, but nine months.
The total is 153.
So let me check if that is basic.
The entire 12 months and not 15 months.
I'll clarify that from my side as well with finance and get back to you.
That's fine, Mithil.
I guess what's our preference, Bridget, between nine months and 12 months?
I don't think I have a strong preference.
Like I really want to avoid doing, not that you're, you know, you're great, but I don't want to do procurement again.
We have someone in that right now where they did separate the contracts and we've had to pause work for like 30 days.
And it's just like, it's just killing the results.
So I don't want to do that.
You mentioned something about seasonality though.
Like what's your concern with nine months?
To be honest, Bridget, there is no concern at all in general.
Like usually what in same kind of situations where we have seen, have done this kind of a bridge kind of a agreement.
Processes have been paused for one week, two weeks.
So there has been a gap kind of similar to what you experienced.
There is no kind of a concern on seasonality.
Usually it's like when we tie it down to entire 12 months, we get a good picture of how things really changed or a period of time.
Seasonality play, it ties very well with our budgeting cycle as well.
So again, it's not a strong preference or anything or any kind of like a deal breaker.
It's simply like, hey, do you want to do it like a 12 month?
If not, we can always go back to nine months, which will technically end around like November 1st.
If we start from November 1st, right?
Yeah.
And sorry, the 153 is the phase two budget.
the phase one plus phase two total is what they have.
Oh, total.
It's the total allocation which Nima has currently.
So they have asked me that, hey, if phase two can, we are okay if that can combine.
We only need to do a like internal solve that is doing two different POs.
So they'll have one PO for phase one and one PO for phase two.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
That's it.
Yeah.
And it's...
Thank you.
It's coming from what they've paid similar vendors in the past.
It's not like a hard, this is the only amount of funds available.
It's this is what they've paid similar vendors in the past.
And I think, Mithil, one thing to consider, there's like more breadth to our service offering.
But I just threw in the chat, like one way to think about the investment.
A lot of clients that we use, they won't use like Profound or like an AEO monitoring platform because of our service.
And we do the strategy and the execution and even the staging and publishing, which is why there's a premium to like what you've paid other vendors.
I understand, Tyler.
And that's the reason.
work.
Yeah.

Any net new vendors and situations like this where we are not able to meet on the budgetary points, we usually go into more kind of a breakout agreements that allows us to actually validate the need of this service, validate the results, and then go back to finance and ask for incremental money.
So that's really it.

Now, based on your justification, everything is like super valid, but there's no kind of a profound data for me to go back to business and basically showcase them that, hey, this is what we have delivered and this is the uptick on what services have been allocated to us by the vendor.
We can also add more layers to it based on resource allocation is changing from the vendor, we are getting more dedicated resources, et cetera.
So all those factors basically provide more kind of like data points for us to go and check in for a higher budget.
It's a solid website.
I understand and I want to make life easy for all of us here.
I think a couple like quick things and then we can close on this point.

Just want to make sure like your team with our portal submission, just that, you know, we're going to be able to get past like the review for the DPA and the addendum, like our team had like a ton of red lines here.
I know we talked about.
About how, like, we wouldn't be ingesting PII or leveraging PII.
So I think, like, we can definitely make a deal here and find a path forward, assuming that, like, our DPA and our addendum is good to be moved forward with, right?
Like, our zip portal is in a good state.
And then I think with the MSA and the SoW, there's just, like, a couple points that we're still waiting to align around, you know?
So, like, to be specific, so, you know, just making sure that the MSA, like, we struck some terms in the SoW that are no longer relevant because the MSA governs it.
With the renewal, we changed the language to be opt-in, right, instead of, like, automatic.
Yep.
So I think if we're good there, like, if we could do quarterly payment terms, like, we'll definitely do our best.
It to be flexible on the budget, but let's just go ahead and make sure that our submission and our legal language is good before we agree on the cost for phase two.
Yeah, absolutely.
So that's not an issue, Tyler, because I'm already working with our security team and everyone.
So I think it's in process of review.
So that is not really obstructing what finances outcome is coming.
They are like, security legal is doing their due diligence on making sure we are able to come to a mutual solution to execute the contract.
With that, finance still plays like one approval.
They are a part of like the final approval workflow.
So even though everything gets signed off, it will go to them for the final approval before the PO is getting issued.
So that's what I'm trying to solve.
I'm not worried about the DPA, everything, all submissions are in.
If there are any red lines, we will get the comments back.
And sometimes our security, if they want to really solve something, they will also go back in the portal and put a comment right away so they can have an immediate solve on.

I have to jump, Tyler's point, I think from our side is like, we need to take into consideration everything before giving a discount because we're going to have to buy insurance or build all these new processes or something crazy.
It's not worth a discount for us.
So we don't want to have that conversation before we wrap up everything else.
So let's do this, Bridget.
And to your point, let me basically circle back with Tyler by tomorrow on where we are.
I will gather more kind of like understanding by end of my day.
Let's keep the finance on pause for now till we solve all our legal and security stuff.
Let's get to the prod and get back to you.
And then we can just do a quick kind of a scrub and proceed on the execution.
That sounds great.
That's perfect.
That's perfect.
And I think our goal, Mithil, would probably be to hit like 12K in quarterly.
So you might want to just check and see if that's possible with finance.
And if it's like a non-starter, then we should definitely cross that bridge first.
I will find the multiple ways.
So Tyler, I.
I'll make this work regardless because I have some pressure from Nima on this.
So don't worry about all that.
I'm just trying to see, okay, how can I solve it at the fastest way?
Okay.
And I have to jump to, I'm sure you do as well, but I'm here to spend as much time with you as you need.
So if you want to spend 30 minutes tomorrow, like that might be easier.
So we're not rushing, but hopefully you just make good progress.
No problem.
Let me get back to you in email once I collect all the necessary information by end of day.
Perfect.
Thanks for your hard work.
Really appreciate it.
Thank you.
See you later.
Bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss contract terms, budget constraints, and next steps for GrowthX's engagement with Mithil's company.](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=11.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Legal and security teams are reviewing MSA, SoW, and DPA submissions; finance approval is pending](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=11.0)
  - [Budget constraint of $153K total for 15 months (Phase 1 + Phase 2) based on previous vendor spending](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=231.0)
  - [Exploring options: 3-month Phase 1 ($30K) + 12-month Phase 2 ($123K) vs. separate agreements](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=231.0)
  - [GrowthX emphasizes broader service offering to justify higher pricing compared to previous vendors](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=733.0)

## Topics

### Contract Structure and Duration

  - [Proposed: 15-month total engagement (3-month Phase 1 + 12-month Phase 2)](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=176.0)
  - [Challenge: Mithil's company typically limits new vendor contracts to 12 months](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=176.0)
  - [Potential solution: Two separate purchase orders (POs) under one contract](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=200.0)
  - [Alternative: Separate agreements for Phase 1 and Phase 2 if budget/approval issues persist](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=265.0)

### Budget Constraints and Pricing

  - [Total budget allocation: $153K for 15 months](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=231.0)
  - [Phase 1 (3 months): $30K approved, possibility to stretch to $40K](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0)
  - [Phase 2 (12 months): $123K remaining, significantly below GrowthX's standard $180K](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=532.0)
  - [Historical context: Mithil's company paid $8K-$11K/month for similar services previously](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=547.0)
  - [GrowthX proposing $12K/quarter ($48K/year) as a potential compromise](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=1005.0)

### Legal and Security Review

  - [MSA and SoW edits under review by Mithil's legal team](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=11.0)
  - [Security team validating portal submissions, including DPA and addendum](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=23.0)
  - [GrowthX made changes: struck irrelevant terms, changed renewal to opt-in, addressed PII concerns](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=860.0)

### Access and Implementation

  - [GrowthX needs access to Mithil's company's CMS (Content Management System)](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=28.0)
  - [Preference to stage and publish directly, but flexible if it introduces complications](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=57.0)

### Next Steps and Timeline

  - [Mithil to gather more information and update Tyler by email EOD](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=1029.0)
  - [Prioritize resolving legal and security matters before finalizing financial terms](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=994.0)
  - [Aim to launch Phase 1 by November 1st (start of Q4)](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [Potential follow-up meeting for 30 minutes the next day if needed](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=1029.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Mithil to collect necessary information and email update to Tyler EOD](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=1029.0)
  - [Continue legal and security review process for MSA, SoW, DPA, and addendum](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=818.0)
  - [Mithil to explore possibility of $12K/quarter pricing with finance team](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=1005.0)
  - [Resolve any remaining issues with portal submissions and legal language](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=818.0)
  - [Schedule follow-up meeting if needed to finalize details before November 1st launch](https://fathom.video/share/4SuGEH4WZp1fvRuqQsGqGYwTX7DCovmN?tab=summary&timestamp=1029.0)

action_items.description: Explore internal options for 2 separate POs (Phase 1 & 2) under single contract. Update team.
Clarify w/ finance if 153K budget is for 12 or 15 months total. Report back to Tyler.
Prepare flexible budget proposal for Phase 2 based on Mithil's finance feedback.
Gather info on legal/security review progress. Email update to Tyler EOD.
Check w/ finance possibility of 12K quarterly payments for Phase 2. Inform Tyler.
